---
title: "tsconv"
generated: true
description: "1-D convolution along the trailing axis."
---

[вЂ№ back to `nitrix.signal.tsconv`](../nitrix-signal-tsconv.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:tsconv -->
```python
tsconv(X: Num[Array, '... C obs'], weight: Float[Array, 'C_out C K'], *, padding: Padding = 'SAME') -> Num[Array, '... C_out obs_out']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:tsconv -->
1-D convolution along the trailing axis.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:tsconv -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `X` | `Num` | `...` Г— `C` Г— `obs` | вЂ” | Input time series, `(..., C, obs)` -- the trailing two axes are channels and observations. Leading axes are broadcast / batched. |
| `weight` | `Float` | `C_out` Г— `C` Г— `K` | вЂ” | Convolution kernel, `(C_out, C, K)` -- standard channels-out / channels-in / kernel-size layout. |
| `padding` | `Padding` | вЂ” | `'SAME'` | `"SAME"` (default): output has the same observation length as the input. `"VALID"`: output length is `obs - K + 1`. |

**Returns** `Num` вЂ” shape `...` Г— `C_out` Г— `obs_out`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen perf:tsconv -->
:::: {.callout-note title="Benchmarks вЂ” nitrix-perf-bench" collapse="true"}
GPU steady-state **125.6 Вµs** (median, L4); up to **12.88Г— faster** than `scipy.signal.correlate`.  [full report](https://github.com/hypercoil/nitrix-perf-bench/blob/main/reports/PERF_TSCONV.md)

::: {.table-responsive}
| case | platform | param | baseline | status | steady (min/med) | compile | mem | fidelity | ratio |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| tsconv | jax-cpu | obs=16384,k=63 | `cupyx.scipy.signal.correlate` | skipped | вЂ” | вЂ” | вЂ” | вЂ” | вЂ” |
| tsconv | jax-cpu | obs=16384,k=63 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 540.9 Вµs / 654.4 Вµs | 85.39 ms | 543 MB (rss) | вњ“ 0.0091Г—tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cpu | obs=16384,k=63 | `scipy.signal.correlate` | ok | 432.6 Вµs / 435.7 Вµs | 459.0 Вµs | 506 MB (rss) | вњ“ 0.0095Г—tol | 0.80x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cuda12 | obs=16384,k=63 | `cupyx.scipy.signal.correlate` | ok | 219.9 Вµs / 230.2 Вµs | 473.32 ms | 0.07 MB (hbm) | вњ“ 0.054Г—tol | 1.77x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cuda12 | obs=16384,k=63 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 124.4 Вµs / 125.6 Вµs | 326.18 ms | 67.31 MB (hbm) | вњ“ 0.022Г—tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cuda12 | obs=16384,k=63 | `scipy.signal.correlate` | ok | 428.9 Вµs / 434.3 Вµs | 458.4 Вµs | 0.07 MB (hbm) | вњ“ 0.0095Г—tol | 3.45x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cpu | obs=4096,k=15 | `cupyx.scipy.signal.correlate` | skipped | вЂ” | вЂ” | вЂ” | вЂ” | вЂ” |
| tsconv | jax-cpu | obs=4096,k=15 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 230.7 Вµs / 241.9 Вµs | 81.56 ms | 533 MB (rss) | вњ“ 0.0046Г—tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cpu | obs=4096,k=15 | `scipy.signal.correlate` | ok | 64.6 Вµs / 85.3 Вµs | 102.7 Вµs | 506 MB (rss) | вњ“ 0.0015Г—tol | 0.28x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cuda12 | obs=4096,k=15 | `cupyx.scipy.signal.correlate` | ok | 226.8 Вµs / 232.5 Вµs | 2.030 s | 0.02 MB (hbm) | вњ“ 0.032Г—tol | 2.22x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cuda12 | obs=4096,k=15 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 102.2 Вµs / 105.6 Вµs | 1.433 s | 67.16 MB (hbm) | вњ“ 0.0048Г—tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cuda12 | obs=4096,k=15 | `scipy.signal.correlate` | ok | 99.7 Вµs / 102.4 Вµs | 131.5 Вµs | 0.02 MB (hbm) | вњ“ 0.0015Г—tol | 0.98x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cpu | obs=65536,k=127 | `cupyx.scipy.signal.correlate` | skipped | вЂ” | вЂ” | вЂ” | вЂ” | вЂ” |
| tsconv | jax-cpu | obs=65536,k=127 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 2.21 ms / 2.51 ms | 102.25 ms | 560 MB (rss) | вњ“ 0.027Г—tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cpu | obs=65536,k=127 | `scipy.signal.correlate` | ok | 1.87 ms / 2.17 ms | 2.61 ms | 506 MB (rss) | вњ“ 0.016Г—tol | 0.85x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cuda12 | obs=65536,k=127 | `cupyx.scipy.signal.correlate` | ok | 232.6 Вµs / 245.9 Вµs | 358.95 ms | 0.26 MB (hbm) | вњ“ 0.11Г—tol | 1.62x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cuda12 | obs=65536,k=127 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 143.6 Вµs / 144.9 Вµs | 306.24 ms | 67.90 MB (hbm) | вњ“ 0.047Г—tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| tsconv | jax-cuda12 | obs=65536,k=127 | `scipy.signal.correlate` | ok | 1.85 ms / 1.88 ms | 1.89 ms | 0.26 MB (hbm) | вњ“ 0.016Г—tol | 12.88x vs nitrix-jax |
:::

*source: bench `fa0a230c` В· nitrix `ff2bf24d`*
::::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:tsconv -->
::: {.callout-note}
Wraps `jax.lax.conv_general_dilated` with the standard
NCW (batch, channel, time) dim spec.  Batches over any leading
axes by reshaping into a single batch dim and unwrapping.
For multi-channel or multi-subject fMRI `(B, C, T)` is the
natural input shape.

This is the **cross-correlation** convention (the kernel is
**not** flipped), as in deep-learning conv layers
(`torch.nn.Conv1d` is likewise cross-correlation).  A DSP
"convolution" flips the kernel about its centre; reverse
`weight` along its last (`K`) axis -- `weight[..., ::-1]`
-- if you need a true (flipped) convolution.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:tsconv -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `C` | *(undocumented вЂ” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `obs` | *(undocumented вЂ” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `C_out` | *(undocumented вЂ” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `K` | *(undocumented вЂ” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `obs_out` | *(undocumented вЂ” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
